category_title: Deaf and hard of hearing children with ASD who exhibit escape-motivated tantrums during difficult tasks
student_description: Charlie is an 8-year-old boy with ASD and profound hearing loss who engages in escape-motivated tantrums (screaming, pounding fists, leaving his chair) when faced with difficult reading tasks. He uniquely needs proactive functional communication training (e.g., signing 'break') to appropriately request delays, task interspersal to reduce task aversion, and pre-selected reinforcers to build tolerance for work demands.

criteria.rationale: Allowing Charlie to choose what he is working for increases his motivation to complete difficult tasks without tantrums over time.
criteria.check: The tutor's long-term plan includes a system where Charlie selects a positive reinforcer before beginning work and earns it upon completion or an honest attempt.
